headlines.description: — Two VAR decisions went against Southampton in its 1-0 loss to Aston Villa in the Premier League on Saturday.

headlines.description: Chelsea's Cesar Azpilicueta scored his first goal this season as Thomas Tuchel marked his first win in charge with a 2-0 victory over Burnley.

headlines.shortLinkText: Bale hat trick keeps Spurs' UCL hopes alive

name: Wolverhampton Wanderers at West Bromwich Albion

headlines.description: Relegation-threatened West Bromwich Albion fought back to claim a 1-1 draw with Wolverhampton Wanderers on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Brom on brink of relegation after draw with Wolves

headlines.description: Striker Michail Antonio scored twice on his return from injury to fire West Ham United to a 2-1 win at Burnley on Monday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Antonio double sees West Ham go fifth

headlines.description: Callum Wilson's double helped Newcastle United to a thumping 4-2 win away to Leicester City on Friday which all but secured Premier League safety.
headlines.shortLinkText: Newcastle stun Leicester to all but ensure safety

headlines.description: Tottenham's slim hopes of Champions League football next season took a massive dent with a 3-1 loss to Leeds on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs' UCL hopes fade after loss to Leeds

headlines.description: — Crystal Palace secured its survival by beating already-relegated Sheffield United 2-0. Whether Roy Hodgson will manage the team in its eighth successive season in the English Premier League remains unclear.
headlines.shortLinkText: Crystal Palace beats Sheffield United 2-0, EPL status secure

headlines.description: Manchester City missed the opportunity to wrap up the 2020-21 title against Chelsea at the Etihad on Saturday as they fell to a 2-1 loss.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea deny sorry Aguero, Man City title party

headlines.description: Liverpool rode a Sadio Mane opener and some heroics from goalkeeper Alisson to a win over Southampton at Anfield on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool beat Saints in quest for top-four spot

name: Brighton & Hove Albion at Wolverhampton Wanderers

headlines.description: Morgan Gibbs-White's late strike earned Wolverhampton Wanderers a 2-1 comeback victory against 10-man Brighton & Hove Albion.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves leave it late to beat 10-man Brighton

headlines.description: Manchester United made Manchester City wait a little longer to win the Premier League title following a comeback 3-1 win at Villa Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd comeback keeps City waiting for title

headlines.description: West Ham United blew their chance to apply real pressure in the race for a Premier League top-four finish as they slumped to a 1-0 home defeat.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham top-four hopes dented by loss

headlines.description: West Bromwich Albion were relegated from the Premier League on Sunday after they lost 3-1 away to Arsenal.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Brom relegated after loss to Arsenal

headlines.description: Fulham were relegated from the Premier League on Monday after losing 2-0 at home to fellow strugglers Burnley.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham relegated to Champ after loss to Burnley

headlines.description: Leicester City beat Manchester United at Old Trafford on Tuesday night to solidify their position in the Premier League top four.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leicester beat Man United to bolster top-4 spot

headlines.description: Danny Ings scored twice on his return from injury to help Southampton bounce back for a Premier League win over Crystal Palace on Tuesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Ings strikes twice as Southampton beat Palace

headlines.description: Chelsea slipped up in their push for a Champions League spot when they lost 1-0 at home to Arsenal in the Premier League on Wednesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Jorginho blunder hands Arsenal win vs. Chelsea

headlines.description: Everton's faint hopes of a top-four Premier League finish were all but extinguished following a goalless draw with Aston Villa at Villa Park.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton UCL hopes fade after Aston Villa draw

headlines.description: Liverpool's top-four hopes are still alive after Roberto Firmino's brace helped them to a win over Manchester United at Old Trafford on Thursday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool beat Man United with Firmino brace

headlines.description: Premier League champions Manchester City rode a Ferran Torres hat trick to victory over Newcastle United at St James' Park on Friday night.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City beat Newcastle on Torres hat trick

headlines.description: A Rodrigo double helped a rampant Leeds United enjoyed an impressive 4-0 victory at Burnley in the Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Rodrigo double helps Leeds thrash Burnley

headlines.description: — Nathan Tella scored his first goal for Southampton in a 3-1 win over already-relegated Fulham in the English Premier League on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Southampton beats already-relegated Fulham 3-1 in EPL

name: West Ham United at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: West Ham United's fading hopes of finishing in the Premier League's top four suffered another setback when they were held to a 1-1 draw at Brighton.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham top-four hopes dented by Brighton draw

headlines.description: — Tyrick Mitchell’s first goal for Crystal Palace helped his team edge out Aston Villa 3-2 at Selhurst Park in the Premier League on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Palace recovers to beat Villa 3-2 in Premier League

headlines.description: Tottenham kept their faint hopes of Champions League qualification alive after a 2-0 home win against Wolves on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Spurs win to keep Champions League hopes alive

headlines.description: Goalkeeper Alisson scored a last-gasp winner to boost Liverpool's chances of Champions League qualification with a 2-1 win at West Brom.
headlines.shortLinkText: GK Alisson grabs last-gasp winner for Liverpool

headlines.description: Sheffield United striker Daniel Jebbison's early strike condemned Everton to a 1-0 defeat on Sunday that could wreck their European hopes.
headlines.shortLinkText: Teenager Jebbison seals Sheff Utd win at Everton

headlines.description: — Manchester United’s protesting fans were back inside Old Trafford — legally this time — and were treated to a wonder goal by Edinson Cavani in a 1-1 draw against already-relegated Fulham in the Premier League on Tuesday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man United's protesting fans treated to Cavani wonder goal

headlines.description: Leeds United's Patrick Bamford and Tyler Roberts scored late on to spoil the party for Southampton fans as the visitors claimed a 2-0 victory.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leeds' Bamford and Roberts spoil party mood at Southampton

name: Manchester City at Brighton & Hove Albion

headlines.description: Ten-man Manchester City threw away a two-goal lead to lose 3-2 against Brighton & Hove Albion in a frantic and thrilling game.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man City throw away two-goal lead in Brighton loss

headlines.description: Chelsea defeated Leicester City in a Premier League top-four clash that saw the Blues keep control of qualifying for next season's Champions League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea beat Leicester to boost UCL hopes

headlines.description: Everton kept alive their hopes of playing in the Europa League next season after a 48th-minute header from Brazilian forward Richarlison.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton beat Wolves to stay in Europa League hunt

headlines.description: Joe Willock scored in his sixth consecutive Premier League game to give Newcastle United a 1-0 win over relegated Sheffield United.
headlines.shortLinkText: Willock scores again to earn Newcastle win over Sheffield United

headlines.description: — Tottenham’s players were jeered by their own returning fans during and after a 2-1 loss to Aston Villa in the Premier League on Wednesday, which could prove to be Harry Kane’s final home match for the club.
headlines.shortLinkText: Tottenham jeered by returning fans in 2-1 loss to Villa

headlines.description: — Gabriel Martinelli and Nicolas Pepe scored in second-half stoppage time to give Arsenal a 3-1 win at Crystal Palace in the Premier League on Wednesday, keeping alive the team’s outside chances of qualifying for European competition next season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Arsenal beats Palace 3-1 in EPL, keeps European hopes alive

headlines.description: Liverpool bolstered their Champions League hopes with a 3-0 win over Burnley to move ahead of Leicester City into fourth place in the standings.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool bolster top-four hopes in win at Burnley

name: West Ham United at West Bromwich Albion

headlines.description: Late goals by Angelo Ogbonna and Michail Antonio secured a 3-1 Premier League victory for West Ham United at relegated West Bromwich Albion.
headlines.shortLinkText: West Ham close in on Europa League spot

headlines.description: Nicolas Pepe scored twice in the second half as Arsenal beat Brighton & Hove Albion 2-0 in their final Premier League game of the season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Pepe sinks Brighton but Arsenal miss Europe

headlines.description: Chelsea secured their place in the Champions League despite a 2-1 at Aston Villa on the final matchday of the Premier League season.
headlines.shortLinkText: Chelsea sneak into UCL despite loss at Villa

headlines.description: Newcastle United's Joe Willock scored for a seventh game in a row as they won 2-0 at already-relegated Fulham on the last day of the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Willock scores again as Newcastle win at Fulham

name: West Bromwich Albion at Leeds United

headlines.description: Rodrigo, Kalvin Phillips and Patrick Bamford all scored as Leeds United ended their season on a high note with a 3-1 win over relegated West Brom.
headlines.shortLinkText: Leeds end superb season with win over West Brom

headlines.description: Leicester City suffered a horrible case of deja vu as their Champions League dream was shattered on the final day of in a loss to Tottenham Hotspur.
headlines.shortLinkText: Tottenham spoil Leicester's Champions League hopes

headlines.description: — No trophy to celebrate this year but clinching Champions League qualification proved a welcome consolation prize for Liverpool.
headlines.shortLinkText: Liverpool caps season recovery by clinching CL qualification

headlines.description: Sergio Aguero said goodbye to Man City in style as his double off the bench in his final match for the club helped them celebrate the Prem title.
headlines.shortLinkText: Aguero signs off in style as City celebrate title

headlines.description: Relegated Sheffield United bowed out of the Premier League with a 1-0 consolation win over Burnley at Bramall Lane on Sunday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sheffield United end PL experience with win

headlines.description: West Ham United secured Europa League football for next season and their best ever Premier League points total after beating Southampton.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fornals double powers West Ham into Europa League

headlines.description: Manchester United's 2-1 win at Wolves saw the team become the first side to go a league season unbeaten away from home since Arsenal in 2003-04.
headlines.shortLinkText: Man Utd finish unbeaten away from Old Trafford
